turns.user_message: I've finally accepted that I'm completely tapped out. It feels like I've been dragging my feet through the same heavy routine for months now. Every single morning starts with this dull ache behind my eyes that just refuses to fade. I try to push through the usual demands, but my brain keeps hitting a solid wall. I just keep telling myself it's only a rough patch, but the weight keeps piling up.

I've finally accepted that I'm completely tapped out, and it's becoming impossible to pretend otherwise. The same heavy routine that used to just drain me now leaves me staring blankly for hours on end. I catch myself circling the same thoughts repeatedly because my focus just won't stick to anything. My patience has completely evaporated, and even small expectations feel like massive hurdles I can't clear. I keep hoping this fog will lift, but it just settles deeper into my chest every single day.

I've finally accepted that I'm completely tapped out, and honestly, I don't even have the energy to fight it anymore. The heavy routine has turned into a relentless grind that drains every last drop of motivation I used to have. I sit here watching the hours slip by while my mind goes completely blank, unable to process even the simplest requests. Every new demand feels like a physical blow, and I just want to unplug from everything and disappear for a while. This isn't just a rough patch anymore, it's a deep exhaustion that has taken over my entire life.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
